---
title: "Exercise 1"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*.

```{r}
library(car)
library(tidyverse)
library(nnet)
library(MASS)
```

```{r}
display_data <- read.delim('../../Data files/Display.dat', header = TRUE)
display_data <-
  display_data |> mutate(
    fb = as.factor(if_else(fb == "Yes", 1, 0)),
    display = as.factor(if_else(display == "Yes",1,0))
  )
```

Model1: predict understanding of display using whether they pass belief test, age and interaction between belief test and age

```{r}
predict_understand_display_model <- glm(data=display_data, display ~ age + fb + (age * fb), binomial())
```

We can see that all predictors are statistically insignificant. fb has the highest odd-ratio. interaction between age and fb has the lowest odd ratio.

```{r}
summary(predict_understand_display_model)
exp(predict_understand_display_model$coefficients)
```

Model2: predict understanding of display using only fb. AIC is lower. the unexplained variance does go up a bit compared to model1.

```{r}
predict_understand_display_model_using_fb <- glm(data=display_data, display ~ fb, binomial())
summary(predict_understand_display_model_using_fb)
```

Find difference in deviance statistics. use chi-sq test to test if this difference is significant.

The deviance statistics is not significant. this means that using other predictors other than fb does not significantly improve the model.

```{r}
model_chi <- predict_understand_display_model_using_fb$deviance - predict_understand_display_model$deviance
chidf <- predict_understand_display_model_using_fb$df.residual - predict_understand_display_model$df.residual
chisq.prob <- 1 - pchisq(model_chi, chidf)
chisq.prob
```

There are some influential points. for studentized residuals, we cannot find any observations that exceed 2.

```{r}
stud_resids <- rstandard(predict_understand_display_model_using_fb)
unique(which(abs(stud_resids) > 2))

cook_dist <- cooks.distance(predict_understand_display_model_using_fb)
unique(which(cook_dist > (4/dim(display_data)[1])))
```

**Conclusion**: The false believe understanding is the strongest predictor. And the model using only this predictor is sufficient. Though, you can use the other one that contains more predictors.
